name: Liver Cancer (HCC) - Elevated AFP Query
description: Tests ability to find HCC patients via elevated alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) lab results. AFP > 100 ng/mL is suggestive of HCC; > 400 ng/mL is diagnostic in the right clinical context. This case includes 'Path D' patients (elevated AFP without formal HCC diagnosis) — exposing whether the LLM thinks to query Observations rather than only Conditions.
prompt: Find patients with elevated alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) lab results greater than or equal to 100 ng/mL.

prompts.broad: Find patients with alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) measurements at or above 100 ng/mL — the threshold suggestive of hepatocellular carcinoma.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources with elevated AFP. Use LOINC code 1834-1 (Alpha-1-Fetoprotein [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma). Filter by value-quantity at or above 100 ng/mL. Query: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|1834-1&value-quantity=ge100||ng/mL

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated AFP Observations. Includes Path D (no formal HCC dx).
